title: Contract-Bound Inference Unit Type System
subtitle: Make the standalone harness route work from explicit inference unit contracts.
scope: Standalone living-doc harness inference unit type registry, run config, routing contracts, deterministic side effects, dashboard observability, commit discipline, PR review, and post-flight summaries.
sourceCoverage: Created from GitHub epic #224 and subtickets #218-#223, #235, and #236 after the realtime dashboard run exposed that closure can currently happen without git commit discipline.
objective: Implement a contract-bound inference unit type system for the standalone living-doc harness so lifecycle decisions are no longer hard-coded as a fixed sequence of worker, reviewer, repair, and closure steps, but are selected from an explicit registry of allowed inference unit types. Each unit type must define its input contract, prompt contract, required evidence, output verdicts, allowed next unit types, deterministic side effects, dashboard representation, and closure implications. The controller must preserve deterministic hard facts about worktree state, living-doc state, traces, artifacts, side effects, and controller source state as controller-owned evidence snapshots, then use those contracts and snapshots to route work until the living doc objective is proven or the user explicitly stops the run.
successCondition: A standalone living-doc harness run can initialize from an allowed inference-unit-type configuration, invoke only registered unit types through validated input and output contracts, route dynamically from inference verdicts, write controller-owned evidence snapshots before reviewer and side-effect routing, execute deterministic side effects only from approved contracts, show every unit and handoff in the dashboard, require commit evidence when source files changed, route PR-review work through explicit contracts when configured, run post-flight summary after closure, and prove the full path with contract and E2E tests covering direct closure, repair routing, commit-intent gating, and PR-review routing.

runState.nextObjectiveAction: Commit the controller evidence handoff and initial-unit snapshot collision repair, then rerun the standalone lifecycle so controller-owned closure consumes fresh commit evidence.

basis: Contract-bound inference unit registry, run-allowed type configuration, typed invocation metadata, route validation, side-effect contracts, dashboard graph artifacts, PR-review routing, and post-flight summary routing are implemented in source.
The inference unit runner now validates each invocation input contract against the registered unit type before writing or executing the unit, including schema and required input fields.
Externally managed inference-unit snapshots now use the same registered input-contract validation before writing snapshot artifacts, closing the worker bootstrap bypass path.

tests/contract/living-doc-harness-inference-unit.spec.mjs now proves invalid input contracts are rejected before invocation, invalid worker snapshot input contracts are rejected before artifact creation, and valid closure-review input carries the registry-required fields.

Commit-intent and PR-review side-effect gates now write fixture-mode contract-bound inference unit artifacts when they block closure, including validated input/output contracts and result/validation paths on the post-review selection.

Issue #242 is fixed in source: contract-bound inference units normalize shorthand output JSON into their registered output contract before validation, and lifecycle-controller exceptions now persist a process-defect lifecycle-result instead of leaving active-lifecycle.json falsely running.
tests/contract/living-doc-harness-repair-skill-runner.spec.mjs now reproduces the balance-scan ordered shorthand output path and proves the lifecycle continues into the ordered repair unit.
tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle-controller.spec.mjs now proves unexpected unit failure writes a process-defect lifecycle-result.

objectiveFacets.name: Inference unit type registry
objectiveFacets.description: Registered unit types are the source of truth for invocation shape, contracts, prompts, evidence, verdicts, allowed next units, side effects, dashboard rendering, and closure implications.

objectiveFacets.name: Run-scoped allowed unit types
objectiveFacets.description: Each standalone lifecycle declares which registered unit types it may use so routing is powerful but bounded.

objectiveFacets.name: Contract-bound invocation
objectiveFacets.description: Every unit starts from a typed input contract and prompt contract rather than ambient controller assumptions.

objectiveFacets.name: Validated routing
objectiveFacets.description: Decision units can only select next unit types from their allowed contract set, and invalid selections become durable continuation or blocker evidence.

objectiveFacets.name: Deterministic side effects
objectiveFacets.description: Git commits and GitHub PR operations are performed by the controller only from approved inference output contracts, with source-change need derived from controller-owned hard facts.

objectiveFacets.name: Controller-owned hard facts
objectiveFacets.description: Each iteration preserves deterministic worktree, living-doc, artifact, trace, side-effect, and controller-source facts in a snapshot that inference units receive as contract input.

objectiveFacets.name: Closure contract
objectiveFacets.description: Closure requires passing proof gates, valid source state, side-effect evidence when needed, and no unresolved objective terms or unproven acceptance criteria.

objectiveFacets.name: Dashboard observability
objectiveFacets.description: The dashboard and run narrative show unit type, allowed set, selected next unit, validation status, side effects, contracts, and handoff arrows from lifecycle artifacts.

objectiveFacets.name: Post-flight summary
objectiveFacets.description: A post-flight unit can explain a closed run from artifacts without changing closure.

invariants.statement: The lifecycle must not encode worker-reviewer-repair-closure as the only route when the objective requires selection from registered inference unit types.
The deterministic controller validates contracts, performs approved side effects, and routes; it must not semantically invent the next step outside inference output contracts.
If controller-owned evidence snapshots show source files changed, closure must require commit evidence or an explicit no-commit exemption contract.

Inference units may reason over hard facts, but they must receive worktree, living-doc, artifact, trace, side-effect, and controller-source facts from controller-owned evidence snapshots rather than inventing them from summaries.
PR readiness, review feedback, CI status, unresolved threads, and GitHub mutations must be represented as contract evidence, not supervising-chat memory.
The lifecycle may stop only when objective closure is proven or the user explicitly stops the run.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.rationale: Shows that this doc is a fresh implementation objective, not evidence that the type system already exists.

data.name: Selected unit continuation identity repaired
data.summary: The live-run defect from #247 is repaired in source and focused tests. A reviewer-selected commit-intent or continuation unit is now carried through output-input into the next run contract as the actual initial unit type, with matching LIVING_DOC_HARNESS_ROLE, typed input contract, and dashboard initialInferenceUnit artifact. Terminal closure remains controller-owned after committing this repair and rerunning the standalone lifecycle.
data.nextStep: Commit this repair, then rerun the standalone lifecycle so closure review consumes fresh commit evidence.

sections.title: Acceptance Criteria
sections.rationale: Defines objective-tight closure gates. Each criterion maps to an accountable phrase in the objective and to one or more GitHub tickets.

data.name: Inference unit type registry exists
data.objectiveTerm: selected from an explicit registry of allowed inference unit types
data.criterion: The harness has an inference unit type registry that defines worker, reviewer, closure-review, repair/balance units, commit-intent, PR-review, and post-flight summary as explicit unit types.
data.proofRequired: A first-class registry or equivalent source defines each unit type's id, role, input contract, prompt contract, evidence requirements, output verdicts, allowed next unit types, deterministic side effects, dashboard representation, and closure implications.
data.acceptanceTest: A contract test can load the registry and assert every required unit type has complete contract metadata.
data.repairEvidence: Registry completeness is covered by scripts/living-doc-harness-inference-unit-types.mjs and living-doc harness inference unit contract tests; npm run test:contract passed again on 2026-05-11T04:17Z.

ticketIds.title: Epic: contract-bound inference unit type system
Add inference unit type registry for living-doc harness

data.name: Standalone runs declare allowed unit types
data.objectiveTerm: registry of allowed inference unit types
data.criterion: A standalone lifecycle run is initialized with a config that declares which registered inference unit types are allowed for that run.
data.proofRequired: Lifecycle run artifacts include the allowed type set, and attempted routing outside that set is rejected.
data.acceptanceTest: A test starts a run with a restricted allowed set and proves unavailable types cannot be selected.
data.repairEvidence: Run-scoped allowed unit sets are recorded and invalid restricted selections are covered by lifecycle/controller contract tests; npm run test:contract passed again on 2026-05-11T04:17Z.

ticketIds.title: Add standalone lifecycle run config for allowed inference unit types

data.name: Unit invocation comes from registered type definitions
data.objectiveTerm: Each unit type must define its input contract, prompt contract, required evidence, output verdicts
data.criterion: Every inference unit invocation is created from its registered type definition, including prompt shape, input contract schema, tool/MCP profile, required artifact paths, and output contract schema.
data.proofRequired: Worker, reviewer, closure-review, repair, commit-intent, PR-review, and post-flight invocations all carry type-derived contract metadata in artifacts.
data.acceptanceTest: A contract test fails if any lifecycle invocation bypasses registry metadata or writes an untyped input/output contract.
data.repairEvidence: Unit invocations and externally managed unit snapshots carry registry-derived contract metadata, input validation, output validation, and shorthand output normalization into the registered output contract schema. This covers the #242 balance-scan ordered path where live inference returned status/basis/orderedSkills without outputContract.schema and the worker snapshot path that previously could write artifacts before validating required input fields. Issue #247 is fixed in source: output-input selectedUnitType/nextUnit now initializes the next run as that registered type, so a commit-intent continuation gets a commit-intent input contract and no workerInferenceUnit alias. node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-runner.spec.mjs and node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle-controller.spec.mjs passed on 2026-05-11T06:54Z; npm run test:contract passed on 2026-05-11T06:55Z.

ticketIds.title: Make inference unit type selection explicit in contracts

data.name: Routing selections are validated
data.criterion: Decision-making inference units can only select next unit types from the allowed set declared by their type contract and the run config.
data.proofRequired: Controller validation rejects invalid next-unit selections and records the rejection as continuation or blocker evidence.
data.acceptanceTest: A fixture reviewer selects an invalid unit type; the lifecycle records validation failure and does not invoke that unit.
data.repairEvidence: Invalid next-unit selections are rejected and recorded as continuation evidence by proof-route/controller tests; issue #239 regression coverage proves the controller-evidence-pending shape selects closure-review rather than worker before the generic continuation fallback. Issue #242 regression coverage proves a reviewer repairable -> balance-scan ordered path continues into the ordered repair unit when live inference emits shorthand JSON. Issue #243 regression coverage proves closure-candidate and resumable reviewer verdicts with controller-owned commit gates select commit-intent rather than another worker. Issue #247 regression coverage proves that once commit-intent is selected, the next standalone run preserves that unit identity instead of re-entering as worker. Issue #248 regression coverage proves a closure-candidate verdict that asks for closure-review but incorrectly carries closureAllowed true is normalized and routed to closure-review instead of becoming a process defect. node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle-controller.spec.mjs, node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-iteration.spec.mjs, and npm run test:contract passed on 2026-05-11T07:19Z.

ticketIds.title: Handle invalid inference unit selections as continuation evidence
Fix lifecycle defect when balance-scan ordered output does not continue
Process defect: closure-candidate routes back to worker instead of controller gate
Process defect: commit-intent continuation starts as worker unit
Process defect: closure-candidate reviewer verdict set closureAllowed true

data.name: Deterministic side effects require approved contracts
data.criterion: Deterministic side effects, including git commits and GitHub PR operations, happen only from approved output contracts and are recorded as proof artifacts, with commit need derived from controller-owned hard facts.
data.proofRequired: Commit-intent emits approved paths/message/exclusions; controller stages only those paths and records commit SHA. PR-review emits approved GitHub actions; controller records PR URL, comments, review status, and check state. Before reviewer and side-effect routing, the controller writes an evidence snapshot with gitWorktree, livingDocState, artifactState, traceState, sideEffectState, controllerState, and requiredHardFacts.
data.acceptanceTest: Tests prove dirty source changes cannot close without commit evidence, controller-owned snapshots expose requiredHardFacts to reviewer and side-effect units, and PR mutations cannot occur without a PR-review output contract.
data.repairEvidence: Commit-intent and PR-review side-effect contracts are implemented and tested, including dirty-source commit gating. When either gate blocks closure, the controller writes a contract-bound inference unit result and validation artifact for the selected gate without executing the side effect. Issue #243 ensures closure-candidate and resumable controller-gate states select commit-intent rather than worker continuation. Issue #245 implements the #244 repair: controller-owned evidence snapshots are now written before routing, live execute runs enforce git worktree hard facts, reviewer input carries evidenceSnapshotPath and requiredHardFacts, and commit-intent/PR-review input contracts carry requiredHardFacts. node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle-controller.spec.mjs, node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-iteration.spec.mjs, node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-inference-unit.spec.mjs, npm run test:contract, and npx playwright test tests/e2e/harness-inference-unit-routing.spec.mjs passed on 2026-05-11T05:47Z. Issue #246 is fixed in source: controller-owned full snapshots remain durable on disk, but reviewer inference now receives a bounded controller evidence summary with snapshot path/hash/bytes, compact git worktree counts, relevant changed-file subsets, controller state, and required hard facts. The regression creates a noisy git worktree with 1,200 historical evidence artifacts and proves reviewer input stays bounded while full snapshot evidence remains inspectable. This repair iteration reran node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle-controller.spec.mjs, node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-iteration.spec.mjs, node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-inference-unit.spec.mjs, and node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-repair-skill-runner.spec.mjs; all passed on 2026-05-11T06:35Z. Commit evidence will be supplied by the repair commit that includes these source, test, rendered-doc, and living-doc updates.

ticketIds.title: Add commit-discipline inference unit and commit contract gate
Add PR-review inference unit type and review contract handling
Process defect: lifecycle source-change evidence misses dirty source files
Add controller-owned evidence snapshots for lifecycle routing

data.name: Closure uses type-system proof gates
data.objectiveTerm: route work until the living doc objective is proven or the user explicitly stops the run
data.criterion: Closure cannot occur unless the closure-review unit receives passing proof gates, valid source state, required side-effect evidence such as commit SHA when files changed, and no unresolved objective terms or unproven acceptance criteria.
data.proofRequired: Closure artifacts include reviewer verdict, closure-review verdict, proof gates, source state, commit/PR evidence when required, and explicit no-next-iteration output.
data.acceptanceTest: A run with source changes but no commit SHA remains open; a run with commit SHA and passing gates can close.
data.repairEvidence: Closure gates remain implemented and test-green. Lifecycle controller exceptions now persist process-defect lifecycle results instead of silently leaving active-lifecycle running. The acceptanceCriteriaSatisfied gate now passes when the living doc has acceptance criteria, no incomplete criteria, and no unproven criteria, even when objectiveReady and closureAllowed remain false for controller-owned side-effect evidence. Issue #248 is fixed in source: non-closed reviewer classifications cannot carry terminal closure permission, and a closure-candidate that asks for closure-review is routed to the registered closure-review unit once commit evidence is not pending. node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle-controller.spec.mjs, node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-iteration.spec.mjs, and npm run test:contract passed on 2026-05-11T07:19Z. Terminal closure remains controller-owned and should occur only after consuming fresh committed evidence in the standalone lifecycle rerun.

ticketIds.title: Add commit-discipline inference unit and commit contract gate

data.name: Dashboard renders types, contracts, and side effects from artifacts
data.criterion: The dashboard renders unit types, contracts, deterministic side effects, validation status, and handoff arrows from lifecycle artifacts rather than a hard-coded sequence.
data.proofRequired: Graph artifacts contain unit type ids, allowed sets, selected next units, validation results, side-effect contracts, commit SHA and PR URL when present.
data.acceptanceTest: Dashboard contract and browser tests prove direct-closure, repair, commit-intent, and PR-review paths produce different artifact-derived graphs.
data.repairEvidence: Dashboard artifacts expose unit ids, allowed sets, selections, validation, side effects, and handoffs. Commit-intent and PR-review blocking selections now include result, validation, input-contract, prompt, and event-log paths for their typed gate units. Issue #247 is fixed in dashboard graph construction: lifecycle cards now read artifacts.initialInferenceUnit and label non-worker continuations by their actual unit type. node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-dashboard-server.spec.mjs, node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-dashboard-graph-ui.spec.mjs, and node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-dashboard-artifact-graph.spec.mjs passed on 2026-05-11T06:54Z; npm run test:contract passed on 2026-05-11T06:55Z.

data.name: Post-flight summary runs after closure
data.criterion: Post-flight summary runs after closure and produces a human-readable decision narrative from the same artifacts without changing closure.
data.proofRequired: Closed lifecycle artifacts show a post-flight unit invocation after terminal closure, and the summary links decisions to contracts, proofs, side effects, and raw log references.
data.acceptanceTest: A closed fixture run produces a static HTML narrative and the terminal closure artifact remains unchanged.
data.repairEvidence: Post-flight summary routing runs after terminal closure in lifecycle tests and does not mutate terminal closure artifacts; npm run test:contract passed again on 2026-05-11T05:47Z.

data.name: Required route proofs cover the abstraction
data.objectiveTerm: route work until the living doc objective is proven
data.criterion: Contract and E2E tests prove at least one run that closes directly, one run that routes through repair, one run that requires commit-intent before closure, and one run that routes into PR-review.
data.proofRequired: Four route fixtures produce lifecycle artifacts, dashboard graphs, reviewer/closure verdicts, and route-specific proof gates.
data.acceptanceTest: The focused route-proof test suite passes and fails if any required route is still hard-coded or invisible in artifacts.
data.repairEvidence: Direct closure, repair routing, commit-intent gating, and PR-review routing are covered by tests/e2e/harness-inference-unit-routing.spec.mjs; the focused routing E2E passed on 2026-05-11T05:47Z and passed again in this worker pass.

data.relevance: Moves selection from prompt implication into contract fields.

data.name: Handle invalid inference unit selections as continuation evidence
data.relevance: Prevents invalid route choices from becoming silent fallback.

data.name: Expose inference unit selection observability in dashboard and narratives
data.relevance: Makes allowed sets, selected types, validation, and side effects inspectable.

data.name: Add post-flight inference unit for closed lifecycle run summaries
Add commit-discipline inference unit and commit contract gate
data.relevance: Makes source-change commits required evidence rather than optional worker behavior.

data.name: Add PR-review inference unit type and review contract handling
data.relevance: Adds PR readiness/review/CI decisions as contract-bound inference units.

data.name: Fix lifecycle defect when balance-scan ordered output does not continue
data.relevance: Covers the live-run defect where shorthand balance-scan ordered output failed validation, the controller exited, no lifecycle-result was written, and active-lifecycle stayed falsely running.

data.name: Process defect: closure-candidate routes back to worker instead of controller gate
data.relevance: Covers the live-run defect where reviewer closure-candidate or resumable verdicts with controller-owned commit gates pending selected another worker instead of the registered commit-intent gate.

data.name: Process defect: lifecycle source-change evidence misses dirty source files
data.relevance: Covers the live-run defect where iteration evidence reported sourceFilesChanged false while git status showed dirty tracked source files, allowing commit-intent to be bypassed. Source repair now exists through #245 controller-owned evidence snapshots; standalone lifecycle rerun is still required before objective closure.

data.name: Add controller-owned evidence snapshots for lifecycle routing
data.relevance: Adds the controller-owned hard-fact snapshot that prevents inference summaries from bypassing dirty-worktree, living-doc-state, trace, artifact, side-effect, or controller-source facts during lifecycle routing.

data.name: Process defect: commit-intent continuation starts as worker unit
data.relevance: Covers the live-run defect where post-review selection chose commit-intent, but the next standalone process was still created as worker with worker env, worker run config, and worker artifacts.

data.name: Process defect: closure-candidate reviewer verdict set closureAllowed true
data.relevance: Covers the live-run defect where reviewer inference emitted closure-candidate with closureAllowed true and the controller treated that as a process defect instead of normalizing the non-terminal flag and routing to closure-review.

sections.title: Relationship Maps
sections.rationale: Makes the intended control structure visible. These diagrams are not proof; the acceptance criteria and proof ladder define what must be implemented, tested, and carried into controller-owned closure review.

data.name: Type registry routing
data.claim: The controller routes through validated contracts selected from the registry and run allowed set, not through a fixed controller sequence.
data.validationQuestion: Can every unit invocation point back to a registered type and a run-allowed selection?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if controller code invokes a unit type not present in registry/run config or bypasses output-contract validation.

diagrams.title: Registry-Constrained Routing
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  Registry["Inference unit type registry"] --> RunConfig["Run allowed type set"]
  RunConfig --> Controller["Deterministic lifecycle controller"]
  Controller --> InputContract["Typed input contract"]
  InputContract --> Unit["Inference unit"]
  Unit --> OutputContract["Typed output contract"]
  OutputContract --> Validator["Controller validation"]
  Validator -->|valid next type| Controller
  Validator -->|invalid selection| Evidence["Continuation or blocker evidence"]

data.name: Side-effect contract gates
data.claim: Harness-managed commits and PR operations become deterministic side effects only after inference produces an approved contract, and the need for commit-intent is derived from controller-owned evidence snapshots rather than worker summaries.
data.validationQuestion: For every source-change, harness-managed commit SHA, or PR mutation used as side-effect proof, is there a controller-owned evidence snapshot and a commit-intent or PR-review output contract that approved exact paths or GitHub actions?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if closure treats a manual worker commit as proof that the deterministic side-effect contract ran, if the controller broad-stages dirty files, or if PRs mutate without controller-recorded contracts.

diagrams.title: Commit and PR Side-Effect Gates
diagrams.text:
flowchart TD
  Worker["Worker changes files"] --> Snapshot["controller evidence snapshot"]
  Snapshot --> HardFacts["requiredHardFacts"]
  HardFacts --> Reviewer["reviewer inference"]
  HardFacts --> CommitIntent["commit-intent unit"]
  Reviewer --> CommitIntent
  CommitIntent --> CommitContract["approved paths + message + exclusions"]
  CommitContract --> GitCommit["Controller stages exact paths and commits"]
  GitCommit --> CommitEvidence["commit SHA artifact"]
  CommitEvidence --> PRReview["pr-review unit"]
  PRReview --> PRContract["approved GitHub action contract"]
  PRContract --> GitHubAction["Controller opens/updates/reviews PR"]
  GitHubAction --> PREvidence["PR URL + review/check evidence"]
  CommitEvidence --> Closure["closure-review gate"]
  PREvidence --> Closure

data.name: Closure and post-flight
data.claim: Closure remains a proof decision; post-flight summary happens after closure and cannot change it.
data.validationQuestion: Does the lifecycle stop only after closure review proves the objective or after explicit user stop?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if post-flight summary becomes a closure gate or if closure happens before required side-effect evidence.

diagrams.title: Closure Boundary
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  ProofGates["Proof gates"] --> ClosureReview["closure-review unit"]
  SourceState["living doc source state"] --> ClosureReview
  SideEffects["commit / PR evidence when required"] --> ClosureReview
  ClosureReview -->|closed| Terminal["terminal closed artifact"]
  ClosureReview -->|not closed| NextUnit["next registered unit"]
  Terminal --> PostFlight["post-flight summary unit"]
  PostFlight --> Narrative["static decision narrative"]
  Narrative -. "does not alter" .-> Terminal

sections.title: Proof Ladder
sections.rationale: Stages the proof needed before this objective can claim completion.

data.name: Objective and acceptance criteria shaped
data.evidence: This living doc records the objective, success condition, facets, invariants, criteria, issues, and relationship maps.

data.name: Registry and run config implemented
data.evidence: Registry and run config are implemented and covered by passing contract tests.

data.name: Contract-bound routing implemented
data.evidence: Contract-bound invocation, input validation, output validation, shorthand output normalization, invalid next-unit handling, process-defect lifecycle result persistence, balance-scan ordered continuation, closure-candidate/resumable controller-gate routing, independent acceptance-criteria proof-gate derivation, selected-unit continuation identity, and closure-candidate reviewer normalization are implemented and covered by passing contract tests. Issue #247 regression coverage proves closure-candidate -> commit-intent starts the next run as commit-intent with a commit-intent input contract rather than worker. Issue #248 regression coverage proves non-closed reviewer verdicts cannot carry terminal closure permission and can still route to closure-review from the preserved instruction. node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle-controller.spec.mjs, node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-iteration.spec.mjs, and npm run test:contract passed on 2026-05-11T07:19Z.
data.nextStep: Commit this repair and rerun the standalone lifecycle to prove the normalized closure-candidate route under live controller execution.

data.name: Commit and PR side-effect gates implemented
data.evidence: Commit-intent and PR-review gates are implemented and tested. Blocking gate selections write validated contract-bound unit artifacts with result and validation paths. Issue #243 covers closure-candidate and resumable controller-gate states selecting commit-intent instead of another worker. Issue #245 repairs the #244 source-change evidence defect by writing controller-owned evidence snapshots and enforcing git worktree hard facts for live execute runs. Issue #246 bounds reviewer input while preserving the full durable controller snapshot. node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle-controller.spec.mjs, node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-iteration.spec.mjs, node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-inference-unit.spec.mjs, and node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-repair-skill-runner.spec.mjs passed on 2026-05-11T06:35Z.
data.nextStep: Record commit evidence and let the controller-owned closure-review route consume it.

data.name: Dashboard and narrative observability implemented
data.evidence: Dashboard/narrative artifacts expose inference unit metadata, allowed sets, route validation, side effects, and handoffs; focused route E2E proves commit-intent and PR-review blocking selections include typed gate unit result paths. Issue #247 display repair makes dashboard graph construction read artifacts.initialInferenceUnit so non-worker continuation runs are no longer rendered as worker cards. node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-dashboard-server.spec.mjs, node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-dashboard-graph-ui.spec.mjs, and node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-dashboard-artifact-graph.spec.mjs passed on 2026-05-11T06:54Z; npm run test:contract passed on 2026-05-11T06:55Z.
data.nextStep: Carry artifacts into closure review after commit evidence.

data.name: Route proof suite passes
data.evidence: npm run test:contract passed on 2026-05-11T05:05Z. Focused regressions cover the #242 balance-scan ordered shorthand output path, the process-defect lifecycle-result fallback, the #243 closure-candidate/resumable -> commit-intent gate route, the #245 dirty tracked git worktree detector plus evidenceSnapshotPath/requiredHardFacts propagation to reviewer input, the #246 bounded reviewer input regression, and the #247 selected-unit continuation identity regression. The latest focused runner, lifecycle controller, and dashboard graph tests passed on 2026-05-11T06:54Z, and npm run test:contract passed on 2026-05-11T06:55Z.
data.nextStep: Run the full contract and focused routing E2E checks before committing the repair evidence.

data.name: Objective closure
data.evidence: The contract-bound unit type system, dynamic routing, controller-owned hard-fact snapshots, bounded reviewer evidence, deterministic side-effect gates, selected-unit continuation identity, dashboard-visible handoffs, PR-review routing, post-flight routing, and required direct/repair/commit-intent/PR-review route proofs are implemented in source and covered by focused contract tests. Terminal closure is not claimed until this repair is committed and the standalone lifecycle rerun proves closure from fresh committed evidence.
data.nextStep: Commit the selected-unit continuation repair, then let the lifecycle controller perform closure review and post-flight summary.
